title: parseModelSpec ownership at the execute boundary
context: parseModelSpec lives in packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts today. It defaults bare model ids to provider='anthropic', pinning the monitor classify path to Anthropic regardless of session configuration. The same function logic (model-id parsing and provider selection) belongs at the execute boundary, which pi-jit-agents now owns per docs/planning/jit-agents-spec.md §2. Keeping it in pi-behavior-monitors creates duplication (issue-043, the forcing artifact) and embeds policy in the wrong layer. pi-workflows has the same structural question for its agent-step dispatch but with different currently-hidden behavior. Widening 2026-05-26: empirical grep verified a SECOND parseModelSpec duplicate at packages/pi-workflows/src/step-monitor.ts:371 (used at line 270) — DEC-0003 originally said 'audit pi-workflows / if found consolidate'; this widening converts the audit-conditional into a confirmed-two-site cleanup. Both duplicates are within scope of the move; neither is to be left behind.
decision: Move parseModelSpec into pi-jit-agents as part of the executeAgent boundary. Remove the CONFIRMED duplicates at TWO sites: (a) packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts:1173 (definition) + 1390 (call site); (b) packages/pi-workflows/src/step-monitor.ts:371 (definition) + 270 (call site). The pi-workflows duplicate is empirically verified (2026-05-26 widening), not merely 'to audit' — DEC-0049 uniform-agent axiom forbids the duplication and TASK-080 dismantles agent-layer copies in the same direction. Update the consumers' classifyViaAgent (monitors) and step-monitor's classify dispatch (workflows) to call the pi-jit-agents API. The function's new location must accommodate DEC-0001 (ExtensionContext lookup for bare specs) and DEC-0002 (thinking-seam enforcement).
consequences: pi-jit-agents gains model-spec-parsing inside the executeAgent dispatch path — as an internal (non-exported) helper at the executeAgent boundary, NOT as a fifth exported surface beyond load/compile/execute/introspect. The four-boundary-surfaces declaration of jit-agents-spec.md §2 stands; the 'either helper / or inlined' hedge in the original consequence is resolved to the inlined-or-internal-helper form by canon (jit-agents-spec §2 four-surface declaration + JI-010 composite-not-decomposition).
pi-behavior-monitors' classifyViaAgent shrinks — it calls executeAgent with a compiled spec and an ExtensionContext, no longer doing its own provider resolution
pi-workflows' step-monitor classify dispatch shrinks identically — the duplicate parseModelSpec at step-monitor.ts:371 is removed; line 270 redirects to the pi-jit-agents API
Requires DEC-0001 resolved before implementation, because the bare-spec semantics determine what parseModelSpec's new contract is
After enactment + implementation: zero parseModelSpec definitions outside pi-jit-agents; verifiable via grep 'function parseModelSpec' returning a single hit in jit-agents only
